supplementEntry.name: Tongkat Ali
supplementEntry.evidenceTierRationale: Meta-analysis of 5 RCTs shows significant testosterone increase (SMD=1.352, p=0.001); 6+ RCTs with clean safety profiles; moderate individual trial sample sizes (32-105)
supplementEntry.optimalDuration: 4-12 weeks for hormonal effects; up to 6 months for erectile/fertility outcomes
mechanismsOfAction: Antioxidant activity (reduced oxidative stress in reproductive tissues)

effectSizes.totalTestosterone: Large effect (SMD=1.352, 95% CI 0.565-2.138, p=0.001) from meta-analysis of 5 RCTs (Leisegang 2022)
effectSizes.erectileFunction: +2.15 points IIEF-5 (95% CI 1.03-3.27) in low-baseline subgroup (Kotirum 2015 meta-analysis)

evidence.title: Effect of Tongkat Ali on stress hormones and psychological mood state in moderately stressed subjects
evidence.journal: Journal of the International Society of Sports Nutrition
evidence.findings: 200 mg/day for 4 weeks significantly reduced salivary cortisol by 16% vs placebo and increased testosterone by 37% in moderately stressed adults (n=63). Significant improvements in tension (-11%), anger (-12%), and confusion (-15%).
evidence.methodology: Double-blind RCT in 63 moderately stressed adults, 4-week intervention, salivary hormone assays and POMS mood assessment

evidence.title: Eurycoma longifolia (Jack) Improves Serum Total Testosterone in Men: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Clinical Trials
evidence.findings: Meta-analysis of 5 RCTs demonstrated significant increase in total testosterone (SMD=1.352, 95% CI 0.565-2.138, p=0.001). Benefits observed in both healthy volunteers and hypogonadal men.
evidence.methodology: Systematic review with meta-analysis of 9 studies (5 RCTs pooled), random-effects model

evidence.title: The effect of Eurycoma Longifolia on the regulation of reproductive hormones in young males
evidence.findings: 600 mg/day for 2 weeks significantly increased testosterone and free testosterone in young healthy males (n=32). No changes in LH or FSH, suggesting mechanism via HPA axis rather than HPG axis in young men.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind RCT in 32 healthy young males (mean age 24.4), 2-week intervention, serum hormone analysis

evidence.title: Effect of Eurycoma longifolia standardised aqueous root extract-Physta on testosterone levels and quality of life in ageing male subjects: a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled multicentre study
evidence.studyType: Phase II randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled multicentre trial
evidence.findings: 200 mg significantly increased serum total testosterone within 2 weeks, sustained through 12 weeks. 100 mg significant at week 12. Reduced Ageing Male Symptoms and Fatigue Severity Scale scores. Increased muscle strength.
evidence.methodology: Multicentre Phase II RCT, 105 males, 3 arms (100 mg, 200 mg, placebo), 12-week intervention

mechanisms.tissueTarget: Hepatic SHBG production and systemic circulation
mechanisms.target: Hepatic SHBG production and systemic circulation

evidence.title: Tongkat Ali as a potential herbal supplement for physically active male and female seniors--a pilot study
evidence.findings: 400 mg/day for 5 weeks significantly increased total and free testosterone and muscular force in 25 participants (ages 57-72). In women, free testosterone increase was likely mediated through decreased SHBG.
evidence.methodology: Open-label pilot in 25 physically active seniors (13 male, 12 female), 5-week intervention, hormone and grip strength assessment

evidence.title: Immunomodulation in Middle-Aged Humans Via the Ingestion of Physta Standardized Root Water Extract of Eurycoma longifolia Jack--A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Parallel Study
evidence.findings: 200 mg/day for 4 weeks significantly improved SIV and immunological grade (p<0.05). Elevated total, naive, and CD4+ T cell counts in 83 middle-aged subjects. No serious adverse effects.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind RCT, 83 completers (40 treatment, 43 placebo), 4-week intervention, immune cell profiling

benefits.specificClaim: Significantly increases serum total testosterone in men
benefits.replicationStatus: Replicated across multiple RCTs and confirmed by meta-analysis

evidence.title: Eurycoma longifolia (Jack) Improves Serum Total Testosterone in Men: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Clinical Trials
evidence.findings: Significant increase in total testosterone (SMD=1.352, 95% CI 0.565-2.138, p=0.001). Benefits in both healthy volunteers and hypogonadal men.
evidence.methodology: Systematic review with meta-analysis, random-effects model, 5 RCTs pooled

evidence.title: Effect of Eurycoma longifolia standardised aqueous root extract-Physta on testosterone levels and quality of life in ageing male subjects: a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled multicentre study
evidence.findings: 200 mg significantly increased testosterone within 2 weeks, sustained through 12 weeks. Dose-dependent effects confirmed.
evidence.methodology: Multicentre Phase II RCT, 3-arm design, 12-week intervention

benefits.specificClaim: Reduces cortisol levels and improves mood in stressed adults
benefits.replicationStatus: Single well-designed RCT with consistent multi-endpoint results

evidence.title: Effect of Tongkat Ali on stress hormones and psychological mood state in moderately stressed subjects
evidence.journal: Journal of the International Society of Sports Nutrition
evidence.findings: Cortisol decreased 16%, testosterone increased 37%. Mood improvements: Tension -11%, Anger -12%, Confusion -15%.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind RCT in moderately stressed adults, POMS mood assessment, salivary hormone assays

benefits.specificClaim: Improves erectile function in men with androgen deficiency and low baseline function
benefits.replicationStatus: Meta-analysis of 2 RCTs with subgroup benefit; supported by 6-month ADAM trial

evidence.title: Efficacy of Tongkat Ali (Eurycoma longifolia) on erectile function improvement: systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials
evidence.findings: Subgroup analysis showed significant improvement of 2.15 points on IIEF-5 (95% CI 1.03-3.27) in subjects with lower baseline erectile function.
evidence.methodology: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 2 RCTs, subgroup analysis by baseline IIEF-5 score

evidence.title: A 6-month, double-blind, placebo-controlled, randomized trial to evaluate the effect of Eurycoma longifolia (Tongkat Ali) and concurrent training on erectile function and testosterone levels in androgen deficiency of aging males (ADAM)
evidence.findings: Combination of Eurycoma longifolia + concurrent training produced most significant improvements in erectile function and total testosterone in men with ADAM.
evidence.methodology: 4-arm RCT (supplement + exercise, supplement alone, exercise alone, placebo), 6-month intervention

metaAnalysisSupport.effectSize: +2.15 IIEF-5 points (95% CI 1.03-3.27) in low-baseline subgroup

benefits.specificClaim: Improves semen parameters and spontaneous pregnancy rates in idiopathic male infertility
benefits.replicationStatus: Single large clinical trial; supported by systematic review evidence

evidence.title: Eurycoma longifolia Jack in managing idiopathic male infertility
evidence.findings: Significant improvement in all semen parameters (concentration, morphology, volume, motility). 11 spontaneous pregnancies (14.7% rate) among completers.
evidence.methodology: Open-label clinical trial, serial semen analysis, pregnancy tracking

benefits.specificClaim: Enhances immune function via increased T cell counts in middle-aged adults

evidence.title: Immunomodulation in Middle-Aged Humans Via the Ingestion of Physta Standardized Root Water Extract of Eurycoma longifolia Jack--A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Parallel Study
evidence.findings: Significant improvements in SIV and immunological grade (p<0.05). Elevated total, naive, and CD4+ T cell counts.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind RCT, 4-week intervention, immune cell profiling with flow cytometry

benefits.specificClaim: Increases muscular force in physically active seniors
benefits.replicationStatus: Single pilot study; not replicated in RCT

evidence.title: Tongkat Ali as a potential herbal supplement for physically active male and female seniors--a pilot study
evidence.findings: Significant increases in total and free testosterone and muscular force in both men and women aged 57-72.
evidence.methodology: Open-label pilot, 5-week intervention, grip strength and hormone assessment

safety.claim: Well-tolerated at 100-600 mg/day across all human clinical trials with no serious adverse events reported

evidence.title: Effect of Eurycoma longifolia standardised aqueous root extract-Physta on testosterone levels and quality of life in ageing male subjects: a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled multicentre study
evidence.findings: No serious adverse events at 100 mg or 200 mg/day over 12 weeks. Clean hepatic and renal panels. Well-tolerated by all 105 participants.
evidence.methodology: Multicentre Phase II RCT with comprehensive safety monitoring including liver and kidney function tests

evidence.title: Immunomodulation in Middle-Aged Humans Via the Ingestion of Physta Standardized Root Water Extract of Eurycoma longifolia Jack--A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Parallel Study
evidence.findings: No serious adverse effects reported at 200 mg/day for 4 weeks in 83 completers.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind RCT with adverse event monitoring

safety.claim: Comprehensive review of safety, efficacy, pharmacology, and toxicology data supports general safety at standard doses

evidence.title: An evidence-based systematic review of tongkat ali (Eurycoma longifolia) by the Natural Standard Research Collaboration
evidence.findings: Comprehensive evidence-based review of safety, efficacy, pharmacology, interactions, adverse effects, toxicology, and dosing for Eurycoma longifolia.
evidence.methodology: Systematic review using Natural Standard methodology, covering clinical trials, case reports, and preclinical data

safety.safetyAspect: Regulatory genotoxicity concern at high doses
safety.claim: EFSA flagged potential clastogenic effects at very high doses (2000 mg/kg in animal models), leading to non-approval as novel food in EU; clinical doses (100-600 mg/day) show clean safety profiles
safety.riskLevel: Low at clinical doses; regulatory concern at supraphysiological doses

evidence.title: Eurycoma Longifolia as a potential adoptogen of male sexual health: a systematic review on clinical studies
evidence.findings: Systematic review of 11 clinical studies (from 150 reviewed) found 7 studies showed remarkable efficacy in treating male sexual disorders with acceptable safety profiles. 4 studies showed insufficient effects.
evidence.methodology: Systematic review of clinical studies published 2000-2014
